Featured Work

Two projects,
one museum.

Illustration · Editorial
01

Children's Design · Illustration

My Little Museum

A children's activity zine for young visitors to the NGMA Bengaluru. Kids follow a bird guide through the collection, discovering hidden objects, reflecting on paintings, and earning an Explorer's Pass at the end. Hand-drawn illustration meets editorial layout, making modern art feel joyful and accessible.

National Gallery of Modern Art, Bengaluru · Curator Approved

Textile · Pattern
02

Textile Design · Cultural Identity

Ceremonial Shawl

A ceremonial shawl gifted to dignitaries of the NGMA. Kannada letterforms reinterpreted as ornamental motifs drawing from the NGMA logo's typographic structure and Bengaluru's cultural identity. Deep navy and parrot green, minimal, memorable, and rooted.

National Gallery of Modern Art, Bengaluru · Curator Approved

About

Illustration that's warm, bold, and made to connect.

I'm Gauri Sharma, a designer and illustrator with a style that's vibrant, playful, and rooted in cultural context. I'm drawn to work that feels alive on the page — the kind kids want to pick up, adults want to frame, and organisations want to hand to people they care about.

I specialise in children's illustration and editorial design, with a particular interest in projects that make complex or important things feel approachable and joyful. Whether it's a zine, a brand identity, a poster series, or a textile pattern, I bring the same energy: expressive, considered, and always human.

Currently interning at the National Gallery of Modern Art in Bengaluru, where my work has been curator-approved and produced for actual museum use. I work online from home and am open to projects in illustration, editorial design, brand identity, and print.
